title: :has()
description: Selects an element based on what it contains — the CSS parent selector developers have wanted for decades. Style a parent or sibling based on its children's state, with no JavaScript.
challenge: When a task's checkbox is checked, give that item a light purple background (`#f3e8ff`) and a purple border (`#a855f7`). Use `:has()` — no JavaScript.
realWorldUsage: Frequently used for form validation UI: style a field's wrapper or label when the input inside has :invalid, without adding a class via JavaScript — .field:has(input:invalid) { border-color: red; }. Also used to style a nav component when a dropdown inside it is open, or to apply a different layout to a card that contains an image versus one that is text-only.
